title: The neighbour across the hall
summary: Describe a neighbour you greet regularly, using present simple and simple physical descriptions.
topic: A neighbour you say hello to every day
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a neighbour you see often. Describe what they look like, when you usually see them, and what you say. Use present simple. You can use 'and', 'but', and 'because'.
hintStarters: I usually see him / her when…
sampleText: My neighbour's name is Mrs Okafor. She lives across the hall from me in my building in Lagos. She is elderly and quite short. She has grey hair and she always wears a green dress on weekdays. I usually see her in the morning when I leave for work. She opens her front door at the same time every day. We always say hello and she smiles at me. Sometimes she carries a small bag of fruit. She never makes noise and she is always very polite. I think she lives alone because I never see other people in her flat. I sometimes hear her radio in the evening. She plays old music and it is very nice. I don't know her very well but I like her. She makes me feel like the building is a friendly place. I always feel better after I say good morning to her.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: at the same time every day
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: vào cùng một giờ mỗi ngày

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third-person singular -s in the present simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi chủ ngữ là 'she', 'he', hay 'it', động từ thêm -s hoặc -es: 'she lives', 'she opens', 'she smiles', 'she carries', 'she makes'. Đây là lỗi phổ biến cần chú ý.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When the subject is 'she', 'he', or 'it', add -s or -es to the verb: 'she lives', 'she opens', 'she smiles'. Don't forget this -s — it's one of the most common A1 errors.

sampleTranslationVi: Tên hàng xóm của tôi là bà Okafor. Bà ấy sống bên kia hành lang của tôi trong tòa nhà ở Lagos. Bà ấy đã lớn tuổi và khá thấp. Bà ấy có tóc bạc và luôn mặc váy xanh lá vào các ngày trong tuần. Tôi thường gặp bà ấy vào buổi sáng khi tôi đi làm. Bà ấy mở cửa trước vào cùng một giờ mỗi ngày. Chúng tôi luôn chào nhau và bà ấy mỉm cười với tôi. Đôi khi bà ấy xách một túi nhỏ đựng trái cây. Bà ấy không bao giờ gây tiếng ồn và luôn rất lịch sự. Tôi nghĩ bà ấy sống một mình vì tôi không bao giờ thấy người khác trong căn hộ của bà. Đôi khi tôi nghe đài của bà ấy vào buổi tối. Bà ấy mở nhạc cũ và nghe rất hay. Tôi không biết bà ấy nhiều lắm nhưng tôi thích bà. Bà ấy làm tôi cảm thấy như tòa nhà là một nơi thân thiện. Tôi luôn cảm thấy tốt hơn sau khi chào buổi sáng với bà ấy.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'She ___ across the hall.'
options: live
living
lives
lived
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does 'she' need on a present simple verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'always' in the correct position?
options: She wears always a green dress.
Always she wearing a green dress.
She wears a green always dress.
She always wears a green dress.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb: 'she always wears'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does an adverb of frequency sit in a sentence?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' correctly to join two contrasting ideas?
options: I don't know her very well but I like her.
I don't know her very well but not like her.
I know her not but like but her.
I don't know but her well very like.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two contrasting clauses, each with subject + verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Both parts of 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes someone's appearance?
options: She have grey hair and she quite short.
She has grey hair and she is quite short.
She is grey hair and quite short have.
She grey hair has and she quite shortness.
mcQuestions.explanation: Use 'has' for possession (hair) and 'is' for states (short) — both with correct third-person forms.
mcQuestions.hint: Which verb is used for possession and which for description?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' correctly to give a reason?
options: I think she lives alone because never I other people see.
Because she alone lives I never see people other.
I think she lives alone because I never see other people there.
I think she because alone lives people never.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' introduces a full clause: 'I never see other people there'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a subject, a verb, and an object.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says someone never does something?
options: She never makes no noise.
She no never makes noise.
She making never noise.
She never makes noise.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'She never makes noise' — one negative word is enough; no double negation.
mcQuestions.hint: How many negative words do you need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes a regular morning routine with a neighbour?
options: Every morning, we say hello when I leave for work.
Every morning I am saying hello when I leaved work.
Morning every I say hello leaving work.
I say hello to her every morning's leaving work.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple for routine: 'we say hello when I leave for work'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for present simple verbs without -ing or -ed.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'sometimes' to describe what a neighbour carries?
options: She carries sometimes small bag fruit.
Sometimes she carries a small bag of fruit.
She sometimes carry a small bag of fruit.
Sometimes carrying small bag fruit she does.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Sometimes' at the start is correct; third-person singular needs 'carries'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check both the position of 'sometimes' and the verb ending.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'and' to correctly join two descriptions of the same person?
options: She is polite and she friendly.
She polite and friendly she is.
She is polite and she is friendly.
She is polite and is she friendly.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'She is polite and she is friendly' — both clauses have subject + is + adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: Make sure both clauses after 'and' have the same structure.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best says how someone makes you feel?
options: She makes me feeling like building friendly place is.
She make me feel the building like friendly.
She makes I feel friendly place building like.
She makes me feel like the building is a friendly place.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'makes me feel' is the correct causative structure at A1: 'make + object + bare infinitive'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'makes me', what form does the next verb take?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Think of one person in your building or street you see often but don't know well. What is one small thing you could do to make that relationship warmer?
